plainEnglish: Don't require a cognitive function test—memorizing a password, solving a puzzle, transcribing characters—to log in, unless you offer an alternative or assistance. Allow password managers and copy-paste, support passkeys or email links, and don't block paste on password fields.
whyItMatters: Users with cognitive disabilities (memory, attention) are locked out by logins that require remembering passwords or solving puzzles with no assistance like password-manager support.
quickCheck: Try logging in with a password manager and by pasting into the password field; confirm neither is blocked and that any puzzle/CAPTCHA has an accessible alternative.
commonMistakes: Blocking paste (onPaste preventDefault) on password or one-time-code fields.
Requiring a CAPTCHA or puzzle with no non-cognitive alternative.
Disabling autocomplete so password managers can't fill credentials.
Requiring users to manually transcribe a code from one place to another.

codeExamples.label: Password field that managers can fill and users can paste
codeExamples.code:
{/* autocomplete lets password managers fill; paste is allowed */}
<input type="password" name="password" autoComplete="current-password" />

codeExamples.label: Password field that blocks paste and autofill
